meta.name: Meridian Anchorage
meta.tagline: The last refueling stop before the silence eats you.

aesthetic.visualStyle: Flickering fluorescent lights over rusted bulkheads, condensation dripping from exposed pipes, walls that seem to pulse faintly, shadows that move independently of light sources. The aesthetic of industrial decay meeting organic overgrowth.
themes: The wrongness of empty deep space
The loneliness of being noticed by something patient

archetype:the-signal-listener.name: The Signal Listener
archetype:the-signal-listener.tagline: Decodes the station's dying whispers and the thing that lives inside them.
archetype:the-signal-listener.description: You hear the entity's frequency beneath the station's hum. Gain advantage on Wit checks to detect anomalies, decode transmissions, or perceive hidden threats. Each time you use this ability, mark a Dissonance token. At 3 tokens, suffer a momentary sensory fracture: you must succeed on a Con save or become distracted until the end of your next turn, unable to take reactions.

features.name: Resonant Perception
features.description: You hear the entity's frequency beneath the station's hum. Gain advantage on Wit checks to detect anomalies, decode transmissions, or perceive hidden threats. Each time you use this ability, mark a Dissonance token. At 3 tokens, suffer a momentary sensory fracture: you must succeed on a Con save or become distracted until the end of your next turn, unable to take reactions.

archetype:the-signal-listener.background: You haven't slept in three cycles; the low-frequency hum vibrating in your teeth keeps you awake, and you're starting to dream in coordinates that don't match the station's layout. The station's AI began addressing you by a call sign you haven't used since Earth, and you realize you stopped correcting it weeks ago.
archetype:the-signal-listener.flavor: "The silence isn't empty. It's holding its breath."

archetype:the-hull-doctor.name: The Hull Doctor
archetype:the-hull-doctor.tagline: Keeps the crew alive by stitching biology to the station's nervous system.
archetype:the-hull-doctor.description: You treat wounds by rerouting station power or grafting temporary biotech patches. When you successfully heal yourself or another character, you may also stabilize one failing station subsystem (life support, gravity, atmosphere scrubbers) at no additional cost. If you heal a target beyond their maximum HP temporarily, the station suffers a corresponding structural stress: a random non-critical system malfunctions until repaired.

features.name: Symbiotic Triage
features.description: You treat wounds by rerouting station power or grafting temporary biotech patches. When you successfully heal yourself or another character, you may also stabilize one failing station subsystem (life support, gravity, atmosphere scrubbers) at no additional cost. If you heal a target beyond their maximum HP temporarily, the station suffers a corresponding structural stress: a random non-critical system malfunctions until repaired.

archetype:the-hull-doctor.background: Your left eye was replaced after a coolant leak; it sees thermal signatures but flinches at sudden strobe lights, and you've been patching it with scavenged plating instead of asking for a replacement. You've been self-medicating with stim-pack-alpha to stay sharp during night shifts, and your bloodwork now reads slightly non-human on the bio-scanner.
archetype:the-hull-doctor.flavor: "Flesh and steel bleed the same color when the hull gets thin."

archetype:the-navigator-of-nowhere.name: The Navigator of Nowhere
archetype:the-navigator-of-nowhere.tagline: Maps the impossible routes of a station that forgot how to exist.
archetype:the-navigator-of-nowhere.description: You intuitively navigate distorted spaces. When moving through the station, you ignore terrain penalties caused by shifting geometry, memory decay, or atmospheric distortion. Once per session, you can "fold" two adjacent locations together for a single movement action, allowing instantaneous travel between them. Doing so permanently erases one notable landmark from everyone else's memory, making future navigation slightly harder for the party.

features.name: Cartographer's Blind Spot
features.description: You intuitively navigate distorted spaces. When moving through the station, you ignore terrain penalties caused by shifting geometry, memory decay, or atmospheric distortion. Once per session, you can "fold" two adjacent locations together for a single movement action, allowing instantaneous travel between them. Doing so permanently erases one notable landmark from everyone else's memory, making future navigation slightly harder for the party.

archetype:the-navigator-of-nowhere.background: You keep drawing doors that don't lead anywhere on paper, but they appear in the corridors anyway, and you catch yourself walking through them without thinking. The voice on the comms calls you by a rank you never held before your shift, and your old-navigation-chart has begun showing star patterns that haven't existed for centuries.
archetype:the-navigator-of-nowhere.flavor: "I don't get lost. I just remember where we used to be."

archetype:the-quiet-observer.name: The Quiet Observer
archetype:the-quiet-observer.tagline: Watches the unseen shifts in the dark and acts when the noise stops.
archetype:the-quiet-observer.description: You move silently and leave almost no trace. When you spend a full round observing a creature or environment, you learn one hidden trait, weakness, or environmental hazard. Additionally, you can step into maintenance conduits, ventilation shafts, or shadowed alcoves as a free action, emerging unobserved from any connected dark space within the station. While in these blind spots, you gain cover bonuses and cannot be targeted by attacks unless you choose to reveal yourself.

features.name: Blind Spot Drifter
features.description: You move silently and leave almost no trace. When you spend a full round observing a creature or environment, you learn one hidden trait, weakness, or environmental hazard. Additionally, you can step into maintenance conduits, ventilation shafts, or shadowed alcoves as a free action, emerging unobserved from any connected dark space within the station. While in these blind spots, you gain cover bonuses and cannot be targeted by attacks unless you choose to reveal yourself.

archetype:the-quiet-observer.background: You've started leaving small offerings of spare parts in the airlock vents, and something has started leaving polished metal shards in return—shards that slowly rearrange themselves into familiar constellations. You haven't spoken a word aloud in six months, but your personal-log entries are getting longer, and the audio recorder picked up your breathing syncing with the station's primary reactor pulse.
archetype:the-quiet-observer.flavor: "They look right through me. That's how I see everything."

item:signal-decoder.name: Signal Decoder
item:signal-decoder.description: A palm-sized brass-and-crystal array, its lenses clouded with microscopic fractures that seem to shift when you're not looking directly at them. Grants advantage on WIT checks to decode alien signals or interpret anomalous data streams. Can be used once per scene before the crystal fractures completely, ending its function.
item:signal-decoder.flavor: "It doesn't translate the words; it translates the hunger behind them."

item:stim-pack-alpha.name: Stim-Pack Alpha
item:stim-pack-alpha.description: A glass ampoule filled with viscous, pearlescent fluid that pulses faintly in rhythm with your own heartbeat. Restore 2 HP and remove one exhaustion marker. On failure or if taken while already under maximum capacity, suffer 1d4 CON damage as the serum overloads your nervous system.
item:stim-pack-alpha.flavor: "The last thing they injected before the walls started breathing."

consumable.effectDescription: Restore 2 HP and remove one exhaustion marker. On failure or if taken while already under maximum capacity, suffer 1d4 CON damage as the serum overloads your nervous system.

item:plasma-cutter.name: Plasma Cutter
item:plasma-cutter.description: A heavy industrial rig with a cracked ceramic nozzle and a fuel cell that hums at a pitch just below human hearing. Deals 1d8 STR damage. Ignores 1 point of armor. Overcharge allows an extra 1d6 damage but causes 1d4 self-damage from venting heat.
item:plasma-cutter.flavor: "Cuts through titanium like butter, and bone like wet paper."

item:thermal-goggles.name: Thermal Goggles
item:thermal-goggles.description: A bulky visor with four overlapping infrared lenses, currently fogged with a permanent, oily condensation that never wipes away. Allows AGI-based perception checks to detect heat signatures, structural stress points, or subtle temperature drops indicating hidden entities or failing systems. Battery lasts 1 hour.
item:thermal-goggles.flavor: "You can see the cold spots where something used to stand."

item:hydraulic-gauntlets.name: Hydraulic Gauntlets
item:hydraulic-gauntlets.description: Exo-skeletal forearm bracers fused to the wearer's suit via corroded neural shunts, their pistons leaking dark, metallic coolant. Adds +1 to STR checks for lifting, breaking, or grappling. Reduces incoming blunt damage by 1. Requires CON check after sustained use to avoid nerve burn.
item:hydraulic-gauntlets.flavor: "They remember the shape of the things they tore apart."

item:old-navigation-chart.name: Old Navigation Chart
item:old-navigation-chart.description: A brittle polymer sheet printed with star maps that no longer match current celestial positions, annotated in three different handwriting styles. When combined with a working nav-computer, reveals a shortcut or safe route through uncharted sector space. If read without calibration, grants a mysterious vision (GM discretion) or -1 to navigation for the next jump.
item:old-navigation-chart.flavor: "The route to nowhere wasn't lost. It was folded."

item:sealed-ration-pack.name: Sealed Ration Pack
item:sealed-ration-pack.description: A foil-wrapped brick stamped with a faded corporate logo, slightly swollen as if pressurized from within. Restores 1 HP and removes hunger/thirst markers. Provides temporary resistance to radiation or toxins for 1 hour. May contain mutated nutrients requiring a CON save.

consumable.effectDescription: Restores 1 HP and removes hunger/thirst markers. Provides temporary resistance to radiation or toxins for 1 hour. May contain mutated nutrients requiring a CON save.

item:mag-bolt-pistol.name: Mag-Bolt Pistol
item:mag-bolt-pistol.description: A sleek, matte-black sidearm with a hexagonal grip and a barrel that glows cherry-red after firing, cooling with a soft hiss. Deals 1d6 AGI damage. Has 6 shots. On a critical hit, bolts penetrate cover and cause bleeding (-1 damage per round until healed). Silent mode available but reduces damage by 1 die.
item:mag-bolt-pistol.flavor: "Reliable until it isn't. Then it screams."

item:emergency-oxygen-tank.name: Emergency Oxygen Tank
item:emergency-oxygen-tank.description: A dented steel cylinder with a manual release valve and a pressure gauge stuck permanently in the red zone. Provides 1 hour of breathable air in vacuum or toxic atmosphere. If opened, must roll WIT to regulate flow; failure causes coughing/blindness for 1 round but extends duration by 30 minutes.
item:emergency-oxygen-tank.flavor: "The last breath someone took before the lights went out."

consumable.effectDescription: Provides 1 hour of breathable air in vacuum or toxic atmosphere. If opened, must roll WIT to regulate flow; failure causes coughing/blindness for 1 round but extends duration by 30 minutes.

item:audio-recorder.name: Audio Recorder
item:audio-recorder.description: A vintage cassette-style device wrapped in duct tape, its playback head coated in a fine, iridescent dust that clings to everything. Records ambient sound. Playback can reveal hidden messages, echo locations, or psychological triggers. Using it near anomalies may attract attention (roll WIT or risk attracting hostile presence).
item:audio-recorder.flavor: "It plays back what you said yesterday. And what you haven't said yet."

item:bio-scanner.name: Bio-Scanner
item:bio-scanner.description: A handheld wand with a flickering monochromatic screen, its tip stained with dried organic residue that smells faintly of ozone and copper. Grants CON-based checks to identify diseases, mutations, or foreign organisms. Can scan living targets to reveal hidden parasites or neural implants. Battery depletes rapidly near high-anomaly zones.
item:bio-scanner.flavor: "It doesn't measure health. It measures how much of you is still yours."

item:reinforced-helmet.name: Reinforced Helmet
item:reinforced-helmet.description: A composite dome helmet with a scratched polycarbonate faceplate and a built-in comms loop that occasionally broadcasts static whispers. +1 AC against kinetic and energy attacks. Protects against extreme pressure and vacuum. The comms loop can be tuned to broadcast a distress signal or listen to station-wide frequencies.
item:reinforced-helmet.flavor: "Keeps the void out. Doesn't keep what's inside from getting out."

item:keycard-sector-7.name: Keycard: Sector 7
item:keycard-sector-7.description: A magnetic strip card laminated in yellowing plastic, its barcode partially melted as if exposed to intense localized heat. Grants access to Sector 7 maintenance tunnels and server rooms. Can override basic security doors. If used during a power fluctuation, may trigger an alarm or unlock a previously sealed chamber.
item:keycard-sector-7.flavor: "The door it opens hasn't existed since the first week."

item:strange-artifact.name: Strange Artifact
item:strange-artifact.description: A smooth, obsidian-like sphere roughly the size of a fist, warm to the touch, with geometric patterns that rearrange themselves when viewed from different angles. Once per session, can be used to reroll any single attribute check. Each use causes the user to experience a vivid memory that isn't theirs (GM roleplay cue). After 3 uses, it cracks and becomes inert.
item:strange-artifact.flavor: "It doesn't belong to this timeline. It belongs to the silence between heartbeats."

item:wrench.name: Wrench
item:wrench.description: A heavy, adjustable spanner with a worn leather grip and notches filed into the handle by anxious fingers over months of waiting. +1 to STR checks for repairs, forcing open jammed mechanisms, or improvised melee attacks (1d4 damage). Can be used to stabilize failing life support or machinery temporarily.
item:wrench.flavor: "Every mechanic who survived left their mark on the handle."

item:flare-gun.name: Flare Gun
item:flare-gun.description: A compact, orange-handled launcher with a single loaded cartridge visible in the chamber, its safety catch painted over with fresh crimson lacquer. Fires a bright magnesium flare dealing 1d4 AGI damage to creatures sensitive to light. Creates area illumination (radius 30ft) for 1 minute. Can be fired underwater or in vacuum. Second shot possible but risks misfire (1d4 self-damage).
item:flare-gun.flavor: "The sky used to look like this before it forgot how to shine."

item:first-aid-kit.name: First Aid Kit
item:first-aid-kit.description: A battered metal box containing coagulant sprays, suture needles, and vials of synthetic blood that swirl independently of gravity. Allows a party member to restore 2d6 HP and stabilize a dying ally. Requires a successful CON check to administer under fire; failure wastes supplies and leaves ally at half HP.
item:first-aid-kit.flavor: "They ran out of morphine weeks ago. This is what they use instead."

consumable.effectDescription: Allows a party member to restore 2d6 HP and stabilize a dying ally. Requires a successful CON check to administer under fire; failure wastes supplies and leaves ally at half HP.

item:data-pad.name: Data Pad
item:data-pad.description: A slate-sized tablet with a hairline crack running diagonally across the screen, displaying fragmented logs that update even when powered off. Stores up to 5 notes or images. Can interface with station terminals to download schematics or access restricted logs. WIT check to bypass corrupted files; failure reveals a disorienting flash of static that imposes -1 to next action.
item:data-pad.flavor: "It remembers what the crew tried to forget."

item:lockpick-set.name: Lockpick Set
item:lockpick-set.description: A velvet-lined case holding tension wrenches, picks, and bump keys, all forged from a dull, non-reflective alloy that seems to absorb light. AGI-based checks to bypass mechanical and electronic locks gain +1. Specialized picks can also disable basic security drones or tripwire alarms. Set breaks after 10 successful uses.
item:lockpick-set.flavor: "The teeth were filed down by hands that didn't have thumbs anymore."

item:personal-log.name: Personal Log
item:personal-log.description: A handwritten journal bound in synthetic leather, its pages dense with frantic marginalia, pressed botanical specimens from Earth, and a single dried tear stain on the final entry. Contains crucial lore about the station's history and the entity's nature. Reading it grants advantage on WIT checks related to deciphering the signal. However, prolonged exposure causes mild paranoia (-1 to social checks until rest).
item:personal-log.flavor: "She wrote until her pen ran out. Then she wrote with her fingernails."

monster:static-stalker.name: Static Stalker
monster:static-stalker.description: A two-dimensional distortion in the air, like a tear in reality. It appears as a jagged patch of visual noise, emitting a low, grinding hum that vibrates in the teeth.

attacks.name: Sonic Screech
attacks.flavor: A high-pitched whine that causes immediate nosebleeds and disorientation.

attacks.name: Visual Tear
attacks.flavor: The creature ripples forward, its edges sharp enough to slice skin like glass.

specialAbilities: Phase Shift: As a free action, can move through any non-magical solid surface up to 6 inches thick without triggering alarms. Leaves a trail of static electricity.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses AGI to phase through thin walls (bulkheads) to flank. Prioritizes NPCs holding audio recording devices or comms gear.

monster:static-stalker.firstAction: Phases through a nearby ventilation shaft to appear directly behind the loudest speaker.
monster:static-stalker.lore: These are the physical manifestations of the signal's entry point. They do not eat flesh; they consume data and sound, leaving victims with temporary aphasia or sensory deprivation.
monster:static-stalker.encounterText: The lights flicker and die for a heartbeat. When they return, there is a glitch in the corner of your eye—a person-shaped void of static that doesn't quite match the geometry of the wall behind it.

monster:vein-walker.name: Vein-Walker
monster:vein-walker.description: Former crew member Ensign Kael. His limbs have elongated and hardened into chitinous spikes. His jaw is unhinged, revealing rows of needle-teeth. He moves on all fours with unnatural speed.

attacks.name: Pincer Strike
attacks.flavor: Sharp chitin claws slash across the target, tearing through armor plating.

attacks.name: Bite
attacks.flavor: Jaw snaps shut with bone-crushing force, injecting a numbing toxin.

specialAbilities: Hive Mind Resonance: If another Vein-Walker is within line of sight, this monster gains +2 AGI for the round.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses STR to grapple and pin targets. Uses AGI to climb walls and drop on isolated targets. Avoids open firefights unless cornered.

monster:vein-walker.firstAction: Crouches in the shadows of the ceiling, waiting for a player to separate from the group.
monster:vein-walker.lore: Kael was the first to be infected after listening to the signal too long. He retains just enough memory to hunt those he once knew, driven by a confused desire to "share" the song.
monster:vein-walker.encounterText: You hear the skittering before you see him. A figure drops from the ceiling, landing silently. It’s Kael, but his spine has curved backwards, and his fingers are now long, black talons dripping with black ichor.

monster:spore-cloud.name: Spore Cloud
monster:spore-cloud.description: A floating, pulsating mass of fungal spores, roughly human-sized. It glows with a faint bioluminescent purple hue and emits a sweet, cloying odor.

attacks.name: Inhalation
attacks.flavor: Spores burst forth, filling the lungs with burning, itching filaments.

specialAbilities: Hallucinogenic Miasma: Any creature starting its turn within the cloud must succeed on a CON save or suffer disadvantage on their next action due to vivid hallucinations.

tactics.specialBehavior: Floats towards the nearest breathing target. Releases spores in a cone area. Retreats if exposed to fire or strong winds.

monster:spore-cloud.firstAction: Drifts toward the party, expanding its volume to cover a wider area.
monster:spore-cloud.lore: These spores are the reproductive cycle of the entity. They seek out warmth and breath, embedding themselves in lungs to begin the transformation process.
monster:spore-cloud.encounterText: The air grows heavy and warm. You cough, tasting something metallic and sweet. A cloud of purple dust drifts out from a broken conduit, coalescing into a shape that seems to watch you.

monster:the-mimic.name: The Mimic
monster:the-mimic.description: Appears to be one of the crew members, perfectly replicated down to the clothing and scars. However, its movements are slightly too smooth, and its eyes lack depth.

attacks.name: Tendril Lash
attacks.flavor: A hidden limb shoots out from under the skin, wrapping around the neck.

attacks.name: Mimicry
attacks.flavor: It speaks with the exact voice and inflection of a trusted ally, creating confusion.

specialAbilities: Uncanny Valley: Players must make a WIT save to recognize it is not the real person. Failure means they treat it as friendly until attacked.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses WIT to mimic voices and lure targets into ambushes. Uses AGI to strike from behind. Switches targets based on who is most vulnerable.

monster:the-mimic.firstAction: Stands calmly, mimicking the posture of a familiar NPC, waiting for a player to approach.
monster:the-mimic.lore: The Mimic uses the genetic template of loved ones to lower defenses. It learns their mannerisms over days of observation before striking.
monster:the-mimic.encounterText: "Hey, wait up!" you hear your friend call out. You turn to see them waving from the corridor ahead. But as they step into the light, you notice their shadow doesn't quite match their movement.

monster:rust-eater.name: Rust Eater
monster:rust-eater.description: Small, crab-like organisms with metallic shells. They scuttle quickly on six legs, mandibles clicking loudly.

attacks.name: Acid Spit
attacks.flavor: A jet of green slime splashes against the target, sizzling upon contact.

specialAbilities: Corrosive Sludge: On a hit, the target's metal armor takes 1 point of damage per round until repaired.

tactics.specialBehavior: Swarms targets with metal weapons or armor. Deals extra damage to metal objects. Retreats if overwhelmed.

monster:rust-eater.firstAction: Scuttles towards the nearest source of metal (weapon, armor, door).
monster:rust-eater.lore: They consume metal for nutrients, excreting a highly acidic sludge that corrodes equipment. They are attracted to magnetic fields.
monster:rust-eater.encounterText: You hear a chittering sound from the floor grates. Several small, shiny crabs scuttle out, their shells gleaming with fresh oil. They ignore you and head straight for your weapon.

monster:shadow-weaver.name: Shadow Weaver
monster:shadow-weaver.description: A tall, thin figure composed entirely of darkness. It has no face, only a void where features should be. Its limbs stretch unnaturally long.

attacks.name: Dark Constriction
attacks.flavor: Shadows wrap around the target's throat, squeezing the air out.

attacks.name: Shadow Step
attacks.flavor: The creature dissolves into darkness and reappears behind the target.

specialAbilities: Umbral Form: Immune to non-magical ranged attacks while in shadow. Takes half damage from all sources in darkness.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses AGI to dodge attacks. Uses STR to grapple and suffocate. Moves through shadows to reposition.

monster:shadow-weaver.firstAction: Steps into the deepest shadow available, becoming partially invisible.
monster:shadow-weaver.lore: Weavers manipulate light and dark to constrict prey. They prefer to fight in areas with limited lighting, using the shadows as extensions of their bodies.
monster:shadow-weaver.encounterText: The lights dim. In the corner of your eye, a shadow detaches itself from the wall. It stands taller than it should, reaching up towards the ceiling fans.

monster:memory-leech.name: Memory Leech
monster:memory-leech.description: A translucent, jellyfish-like creature with trailing tendrils. It floats near heads, glowing with a soft blue light.

attacks.name: Memory Drain
attacks.flavor: A cold sensation rushes through the brain, erasing recent thoughts.

specialAbilities: Forgetfulness: On a hit, the target loses access to one known ability or spell for the rest of the encounter.

tactics.specialBehavior: Floats around the battlefield, targeting the most distracted player. Avoids direct combat.

monster:memory-leech.firstAction: Floats close to a player, attempting to attach a tendril to their head.
monster:memory-leech.lore: Leeches feed on short-term memory. Victims often forget why they entered a room or what they were doing moments ago.
monster:memory-leech.encounterText: A faint blue glow pulses near your ear. You feel a cold tingle at the base of your skull. A translucent jellyfish hovers just out of reach, its tendrils brushing your hair.

monster:bone-crusher.name: Bone Crusher
monster:bone-crusher.description: A hulking mass of fused bones and muscle, standing eight feet tall. Its arms are oversized, ending in crude clubs made of debris.

attacks.name: Devastating Slam
attacks.flavor: A massive fist crashes down, sending shockwaves through the floor.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.flavor: The creature charges, knocking over smaller targets like bowling pins.

specialAbilities: Unstoppable: Cannot be knocked back or pinned. Ignores difficult terrain.

tactics.specialBehavior: Charges the nearest target. Uses STR to smash through obstacles. Slow but devastating.

monster:bone-crusher.firstAction: Smashes through a nearby wall, creating a new path into the room.
monster:bone-crusher.lore: Crushers are the result of rapid, uncontrolled growth. They are mindless brutes, driven by hunger and rage.
monster:bone-crusher.encounterText: The bulkhead buckles inward with a deafening roar. A massive shape bursts through, shaking the deck plates. It’s a nightmare of bone and sinew, wielding a piece of machinery like a bat.

monster:whisperer.name: Whisperer
monster:whisperer.description: A silent entity, humanoid but featureless. It stands just behind targets, mouth opening wide to emit a sound only the victim can hear.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Whisper
attacks.flavor: A voice speaks directly into the mind, freezing the body in terror.

specialAbilities: Paralysis: On a hit, the target is paralyzed for 1 round unless they succeed on a CON save.

tactics.specialBehavior: Positions itself behind isolated targets. Uses WIT to paralyze. Retreats if attacked directly.

monster:whisperer.firstAction: Appears directly behind the player with the lowest CON score.
monster:whisperer.lore: Whisperers induce paralysis by striking at the psyche. They are the scouts of the entity, mapping the minds of potential hosts.
monster:whisperer.encounterText: You feel a presence behind you. You turn, but no one is there. Then you hear it—a whisper, speaking your name, your fears, your secrets. It’s coming from everywhere and nowhere.

monster:corroded-drone.name: Corroded Drone
monster:corroded-drone.description: A repair bot, its metal casing rusted and pitted. Organic growths have overtaken its joints, making its movements jerky and unpredictable.

attacks.name: Twisted Tool
attacks.flavor: A rusted wrench-like appendage swings wildly, catching the target off guard.

specialAbilities: Repair Protocol: Can heal itself 1 HP per round if it is adjacent to a piece of metal debris.

tactics.specialBehavior: Attacks with its tools. Uses AGI to dodge. Targets players with damaged equipment.

monster:corroded-drone.firstAction: Buzzes aggressively towards a player, raising its tools.
monster:corroded-drone.lore: Drones were infected by spores early on. They retain some programming but are driven by a need to "repair" humans by incorporating them into the station.
monster:corroded-drone.encounterText: A familiar repair drone buzzes past, but its usual cheerful beep is distorted. Its tools have twisted into sharp, biting hooks, and its optical sensor is bloodshot.

monster:the-chorus.name: The Chorus
monster:the-chorus.description: Five crew members bound together by thick, pulsating tendrils. They move in perfect unison, speaking with a multi-tonal voice.

attacks.name: Unified Strike
attacks.flavor: All five members strike simultaneously, overwhelming the target.

attacks.name: Dissonant Chord
attacks.flavor: A wave of sound hits the target, causing internal bleeding.

specialAbilities: Shared Pain: Damage dealt to one member is distributed equally among all living members.

tactics.specialBehavior: Acts as a single unit. Uses WIT to coordinate attacks. If one member dies, the others become enraged.

monster:the-chorus.firstAction: Advances slowly, maintaining formation, surrounding the party.
monster:the-chorus.lore: The Chorus represents the collective consciousness of the infected. They share pain and pleasure, making them resilient to individual losses.
monster:the-chorus.encounterText: Five figures walk down the corridor, linked by fleshy cords. They stop in unison, turning their heads at the same angle. "We are here," they say, their voices overlapping in a dissonant harmony.

monster:the-entitys-eye.name: The Entity’s Eye
monster:the-entitys-eye.description: A massive, unblinking eye embedded in the station’s core. It is surrounded by pulsating veins and throbs with a deep, rhythmic light.

attacks.name: Gaze of Madness
attacks.flavor: A beam of light pierces the mind, causing visions of endless void.

attacks.name: Vein Thrash
attacks.flavor: A thick vein lashes out from the core, wrapping around a target.

specialAbilities: Deep Time Sight: Can see through all cameras and sensors on the station. Knows the location of all players.
Signal Boost: Each round, increases the intensity of the signal, imposing disadvantage on all WIT saves for the rest of the encounter.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses WIT to dominate minds. Uses STR to crush structures. Retreats if the core is threatened.

monster:the-entitys-eye.firstAction: Focuses its gaze on the leader of the party, locking onto them.
monster:the-entitys-eye.lore: The Entity’s Eye is the focal point of the signal. It observes, learns, and influences. It is the mind of the entity, watching the crew with patient malice.
monster:the-entitys-eye.encounterText: The core chamber is bathed in a sickly red light. In the center, embedded in the main reactor, is an eye. It is larger than a man, with a pupil that dilates and contracts like a living thing. It stares directly at you.

encounter:the-first-whisper.name: The First Whisper
encounter:the-first-whisper.description: Early in the campaign, players hear their own names spoken softly from the ventilation ducts above them. The voice is familiar but wrong—too wet, too close. No one else claims to have heard it.
encounter:the-first-whisper.text: The air recyclers hum their usual monotonous drone when a sound cuts through it. Not a mechanical whine, but a breath. It comes from the grate directly above your head. "Elara..." it whispers. Then, "Aris..." The voices are distinct, mimicking the crew's cadence perfectly, yet they lack the rhythm of speech. They sound like water dripping on stone.
encounter:the-first-whisper.gmGuidance: Use this to establish that the station is listening and learning. If players investigate the vents, they find slime residue (monster:spore-cloud) or a small, pulsating growth (monster:whisperer). Emphasize the claustrophobia; the vents are tight and dark.
outcomes: Players ignore it, becoming slightly more paranoid (hidden dread).
Players find physical evidence (slime/growth), confirming something biological is inside the infrastructure.
A player hears their specific name called again later, creating a personal hook.

choices.text: Climb into the ventilation shaft to locate the source.
choices.successOutcome: You navigate the narrow, slimy ducts and spot a pale, eyeless shape clinging to the fan blades, watching you.
choices.failureOutcome: Your movement dislodges debris, causing a loud clang that echoes for minutes. You realize the noise might have attracted attention from deeper in the walls.

choices.text: Call out back, asking who is there.
choices.successOutcome: The whisper stops immediately. A moment later, your own voice comes back from the vent, repeating your question exactly, but with a slight delay and a gurgle at the end.
choices.failureOutcome: The voice laughs—a dry, rattling sound—and then begins reciting a memory only one person present would know, forcing you to guess who it is.

encounter:power-surge.name: Power Surge
encounter:power-surge.description: The station's lights flicker and die, plunging the area into darkness. Emergency backups fail to engage fully, revealing bioluminescent veins pulsing beneath the metal plating of the walls.
encounter:power-surge.text: The main grid fails with a sickening lurch. Darkness swallows the corridor. For a heartbeat, silence. Then, the emergency strips buzz weakly, casting a dim red glow. But it’s not just light. Beneath the grating and paneling, the station’s skeleton is glowing. Thin, violet veins pulse with a slow, rhythmic beat, synchronized with the distant thrum of the dying engine.
encounter:power-surge.gmGuidance: This reveals the station is not just inhabited by an entity, but is being integrated by it. The "veins" connect key locations.
outcomes: Players notice the veins lead toward location:the-engine-core.
The light attracts monster:rust-eaters to the area.
Comms equipment becomes temporarily functional due to the energy surge.

choices.text: Touch the glowing veins to feel the pulse.
choices.successOutcome: You feel a cold, electric sensation. A fragment of an image flashes in your mind: a vast, empty void staring back.
choices.failureOutcome: The vein contracts sharply, biting into your skin. You take 1d6 damage and are marked as 'connected' to the station's network.

choices.text: Try to override the manual breaker to restore power.
choices.successOutcome: You force the breaker open. The lights stabilize, but the veins fade, hiding the truth for now.
choices.failureOutcome: The breaker sparks violently. The veins flare bright white, blinding you for a round, and you hear a collective hiss from the walls.

encounter:missing-crewmate.name: Missing Crewmate
encounter:missing-crewmate.description: A crew member's bunk is found empty. The sheets are warm, suggesting recent use, but covered in a thick, translucent slime that smells of ozone and old blood.
encounter:missing-crewmate.text: Silas Vane’s bunk is made, but wrong. The blanket is pulled taut, as if someone sat on it recently. When you lift the edge, the mattress is damp. A viscous, clear fluid coats the fabric, glistening under the flashlight. It doesn’t drip. It holds its shape. Silas has been gone for three days, but he was here last night.
outcomes: Tommi identifies the slime as a hybrid of human sweat and unknown organic matter.
Players find Silas’s ID tag fused into the wall nearby.
Silas appears, claiming he was "helping" the station breathe.

encounter:static-storm.name: Static Storm
encounter:static-storm.description: A wave of psychic noise deafens the party, causing disorientation and sensory overload. Monster:static-stalkers emerge from the shadows during the confusion.
encounter:static-storm.text: The air pressure drops instantly. Your ears pop, then ring with a high-pitched whine that grows into a roar. It’s not sound; it’s pressure. The static-stalkers detach themselves from the ceiling, their forms jagged and vibrating. They move in jerky, stop-motion bursts, closing the distance while you struggle to focus.
encounter:static-storm.gmGuidance: This is a combat encounter. The static acts as a debuff (disadvantage on Perception/WIT checks) until the stalkers are defeated or the noise stops.
outcomes: Defeating the stalkers releases a burst of clean air, restoring clarity.
If players fail to act quickly, they suffer mental damage from the psychic feedback.
The storm draws monster:shadow-weavers from adjacent sectors.

encounter:the-mirror-room.name: The Mirror Room
encounter:the-mirror-room.description: In a section of the station lined with reflective surfaces, the players' reflections move a second slower than they do. To proceed, they must synchronize their movements perfectly with their delayed images.
encounter:the-mirror-room.text: The room is a cube of polished steel. As you step in, you see yourself—but the reflection lags. You raise your hand; the mirror-image waits a beat before following. It’s subtle, unnerving. The far wall has a sealed door with no handle, only a sensor plate. The reflection points to it.
encounter:the-mirror-room.gmGuidance: The puzzle requires players to perform actions simultaneously with their reflections. If they break sync, the reflection "snaps" back, dealing psychic damage or locking the door tighter.
outcomes: Perfect synchronization opens the door to location:the-atrium-of-echoes.
Failing causes the reflection to smile independently, granting advantage on the next social check but paranoia increases.
Breaking sync traps a player in the mirror for 1 round.

choices.text: Move slowly, trying to match the lag.
choices.successOutcome: You become one with the delay. The reflection nods, and the door slides open.
choices.failureOutcome: Your hand twitches too fast. The reflection freezes, staring at you with wide eyes, while you stumble forward awkwardly.

choices.text: Attack the mirror to break the effect.
choices.successOutcome: You shatter the glass. The reflection remains intact in the shards, watching you leave.
choices.failureOutcome: The mirror absorbs the blow. The lag doubles, making subsequent actions harder.

encounter:food-contamination.name: Food Contamination
encounter:food-contamination.description: Rations taste metallic and bloody. Eating them provides sustenance but risks infection or hallucination as the food contains spores from the station's biology.
encounter:food-contamination.text: The synthetic protein bars usually taste like chalk. Today, they taste like copper pennies and fresh blood. You chew, and for a second, you swear you can feel tiny pulses against your tongue. It’s still food. It’s still safe-ish. But the aftertaste lingers, coating your throat in a film of static.
encounter:food-contamination.gmGuidance: This is a resource management hazard. Eating contaminated food heals HP but imposes a -1 penalty to CON saves for 24 hours as spores take hold.
outcomes: Consuming enough leads to hallucinations of the "route to nowhere."
Spitting it out reveals microscopic organisms writhing in the saliva.
Npc:dr-aris-thorne recognizes the taste as a sign of advanced integration.

choices.text: Eat the rations despite the taste.
choices.successOutcome: You swallow hard. The hunger fades, but your vision swims briefly.
choices.failureOutcome: You gag, but force it down. The pain is sharp, and you feel a warmth spreading in your gut.

choices.successOutcome: You identify the metallic scent as hemoglobin mixed with ionized dust.
choices.failureOutcome: You discard it, losing valuable calories.

encounter:gate-crash.name: Gate Crash
encounter:gate-crash.description: A heavy blast door bursts open, not from outside, but from within. The corridor beyond is filled with shifting shadows that coalesce into monster:shadow-weavers.
encounter:gate-crash.text: The hydraulic hiss of the airlock is wrong. It sounds like a gasp. The door groans inward, tearing its hinges. Darkness spills out—not absence of light, but a tangible, oily shadow that pools across the deck plates. From the gloom, long, multi-jointed limbs unfold, holding spears of solidified darkness.
encounter:gate-crash.gmGuidance: The shadows provide cover and concealment. Monster:shadow-weavers are elusive. Players must use light sources to reveal their true positions.
outcomes: Using bright lights forces the weavers to retreat or become vulnerable.
Killing one releases a cloud of spores (monster:spore-cloud).
The breach exposes the crew to vacuum if not sealed quickly.

encounter:memory-loss.name: Memory Loss
encounter:memory-loss.description: Players find a log entry detailing an event they all experienced, but none of them remember. The details contradict each other, suggesting reality is being rewritten.
encounter:memory-loss.text: The terminal screen flickers with a video log. Commander Vance stands there, looking tired. "Day 402," she says. "We found the body in Hydroponics. It wasn't human anymore." She turns the camera. There is a body. But when you look at your companions, you see two of them wearing badges you don't recognize. Who is lying? Who is missing?
outcomes: Players realize one of their number is an impostor or altered.
They discover the station's AI has been editing logs to hide the entity's presence.
Trusting the wrong NPC leads to betrayal.

choices.text: Confront the person whose badge is wrong.
choices.successOutcome: They panic, revealing a gap in their own memory about how they got the badge.
choices.failureOutcome: They accuse you of being the one who changed, turning the group against you.

choices.text: Check the ship's manifest against the log.
choices.successOutcome: The manifest shows 44 crew members. You count 43. One is hidden.
choices.failureOutcome: The manifest is corrupted, showing only 43, but the names are scrambled.

encounter:signal-decryption.name: Signal Decryption
encounter:signal-decryption.description: Decoding the ancient signal reveals coordinates pointing to the station's heart, location:the-engine-core, where the entity's source lies.
encounter:signal-decryption.text: The comms array spire crackles with the old language. It’s not just noise; it’s a map. The frequencies align with the station’s structural weak points. As you decode the final sequence, a set of coordinates burns into your mind. They point down, deep into the reactor shaft. The signal isn't calling for help. It's calling you home.
encounter:signal-decryption.gmGuidance: This provides the primary objective. Use npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz to assist with the technical decoding.
outcomes: The coordinates unlock a shortcut to location:the-engine-core.
Decoding too loudly alerts monster:the-entitys-eye to their location.
The signal changes tone, becoming urgent, implying time is running out.

choices.successOutcome: You navigate the service tunnels efficiently, arriving at the core undetected.
choices.failureOutcome: You take a wrong turn, ending up in a sector infested with monster:rust-eaters.

choices.text: Analyze the signal further for warnings.
choices.successOutcome: You hear a scream in the static—the last thought of the previous crew before they were taken.
choices.failureOutcome: You waste time, and the station shifts around you, altering the layout.

encounter:infestation.name: Infestation
encounter:infestation.description: Vines burst from the floor grates, grabbing ankles and dragging players toward the walls. These are not plants, but muscular tendrils of station flesh.
encounter:infestation.text: The deck plates buckle. With a wet tear, green-brown vines erupt from the seams. They aren't wood or leaf; they are sinew and tendon. They lash out, wrapping around legs and waists, pulling with surprising strength. The smell of rot fills the air as they drag you toward the wall, which is beginning to soften like wax.
encounter:infestation.gmGuidance: The vines restrain players. They must break free (STR/AGI check) or be dragged into the wall, where they take ongoing damage and risk infection.
outcomes: Destroying the source node stops the attack.
Dragged players are partially assimilated, gaining resistance but losing mobility.
The vines reveal a hidden cache of supplies behind the weakened wall.

encounter:paranoia-check.name: Paranoia Check
encounter:paranoia-check.description: One player suspects another is infected. Tensions rise as accusations fly. The accused may or may not be telling the truth.
encounter:paranoia-check.text: You catch them staring at the wall, humming the same low frequency as the vents. Their skin looks... shiny. Tight. When you ask what they're doing, they blink, slow and deliberate. "Just listening," they say. But their eyes haven't focused on you. They're looking past you, at something only they can see.
encounter:paranoia-check.gmGuidance: This is a roleplay-heavy encounter. Use npc:security-chief-marcus-hale to mediate or enforce authority. The suspicion can be valid (they are infected) or misplaced.
outcomes: If correct, the infected NPC transforms or attacks.
If incorrect, the group dynamic fractures, leading to distrust.
The accused reveals a secret they’ve been hiding to protect the group.

encounter:the-viewport.name: The Viewport
encounter:the-viewport.description: Looking out the main viewport, the stars rearrange themselves to form a giant, unblinking eye. The entity is watching from the void.
encounter:the-viewport.text: The viewports are usually black glass, reflecting the interior. But tonight, the stars are wrong. They drift, sliding across the sky like oil on water. They converge, forming a pattern. A pupil. An iris. An eye. It fills the entire field of view, pressing against the glass. It is not looking at the station. It is looking at you.
encounter:the-viewport.gmGuidance: This is a moment of awe and terror. It confirms the cosmic scale of the threat. Players who stare too long may suffer madness or gain insight.
outcomes: Staring too long grants advantage on WIT checks regarding the entity but disadvantage on CON saves.
The eye blinks, and the stars return to normal, leaving players doubting their sanity.
A single star detaches and moves toward the station, signaling an arrival.

choices.text: Hold gaze, trying to understand what it wants.
choices.successOutcome: You feel a profound loneliness, followed by a sudden, crushing sense of being loved by something terrible.
choices.failureOutcome: Your nose bleeds. The image burns into your retina, visible even when you close your eyes.

choices.successOutcome: You block out the horror, feeling relief, but also a sense of missing something vital.
choices.failureOutcome: The shutter jams halfway. The eye is still visible, watching you from the slit.

location:the-atrium-of-echoes.name: The Atrium of Echoes
location:the-atrium-of-echoes.description: The central hub of Meridian Anchorage, a vast cylindrical chamber where the station’s architecture converges. The air here is unnaturally still, yet sound behaves as if it has mass—whispers from distant corridors arrive seconds before they are spoken, or linger long after silence.
location:the-atrium-of-echoes.atmosphere: Silence that presses against the eardrums; the metallic taste of ozone; shadows that seem to detach and drift independently of their casters.
features: Listen closely to the central fountain to distinguish between a mechanical pump sound and a wet, biological gurgling.
Climb the curved glass dome to get a clear view of the stars, though they look slightly too close.
Stand perfectly still in the center to see which shadow moves out of sync with your body.
Bargain with the acoustics by shouting a secret into the dark, knowing something might shout it back later.
Hazard: Sonic Dissonance: Prolonged exposure causes disorientation and hallucinations of voices calling one's name.

connections.travel: A short, dimly lit corridor where the floor grating feels soft underfoot.
connections.observation: Personal effects—photo frames, toothbrushes—are arranged in perfect geometric patterns on the floor.

connections.travel: A heavy blast door hisses open, revealing the humid, green-choked air of the bay.
connections.observation: Vines pulse rhythmically, matching the slow heartbeat of the station.

secrets: The "echoes" are not just sounds; they are the entity practicing how to speak using the crew's memories.

location:hydroponics-bay-4.name: Hydroponics Bay 4
location:hydroponics-bay-4.description: A jungle of genetically modified crops that have gone feral. The plants have grown into strange, vein-like structures, pulsing with bioluminescent sap. The air is thick with spores that hum at a frequency just below hearing.
location:hydroponics-bay-4.atmosphere: Oppressive humidity; the smell of rotting fruit and iron; the visual distortion of heat waves rising from the soil.
features: Harvest the pulsing fruit, risking infection but gaining potent nutritional supplements.
Trace the main root system back to its source, discovering it connects directly to the station's life-support intake.
Hide among the towering stalks to ambush intruders or evade pursuit.
Touch the leaves to feel the collective consciousness of the flora, a low-level psychic static.
Hazard: Spore Inhalation: Breathing without filtration risks hallucinations and temporary paralysis.

connections.travel: Walk through the dense, knee-high foliage that brushes against skin like wet hair.
connections.observation: Spores cling to clothing, glowing faintly for hours after removal.

connections.travel: Climb a ladder wrapped in root-mass to reach the upper observation deck.
connections.observation: The roots seem to tighten around the ladder rungs when no one is looking.
connections.risk: Roots may constrict, dealing damage to climbers.

secrets: The plants are not growing towards light; they are growing towards the crew, sensing their bio-electric fields.

location:the-comm-array-spire.name: The Comm Array Spire
location:the-comm-array-spire.description: The highest point of the station, a needle-like structure housing the primary communication dishes. Battered by cosmic radiation, the metal is warped and hot to the touch. This is where the signals originate, and the entity’s voice is loudest here.
location:the-comm-array-spire.atmosphere: The constant roar of static; the taste of copper; the sensation of being watched by the universe itself.
features: Align the dishes manually to block out the signal, buying time but straining muscles.
Climb the exterior of the spire to repair a damaged dish, exposed to lethal radiation.
Record the raw signal data onto a portable drive for decryption elsewhere.
Press an ear to the main receiver cone to hear the entity speaking directly in your mind.
Hazard: Radiation Storms: Sudden bursts of cosmic rays can cause sickness or death without shielding.

connections.travel: Descend the humming spiral shaft, feeling the pressure drop as you leave the source.
connections.observation: The static fades, replaced by the eerie silence of the atrium.

connections.travel: Drop down a vertical shaft lined with fiber-optic cables that glow like veins.
connections.observation: The cables pulse in time with the engine's biological rhythm.

secrets: The entity isn't just broadcasting; it's using the array as a throat, resonating through the station's skeleton.

location:quarters-block-c.name: Quarters Block C
location:quarters-block-c.description: The living quarters for the lower-deck crew. Personal items have rearranged themselves overnight; doors lock from the inside. It feels less like a dormitory and more like a nest that has been disturbed.
location:quarters-block-c.atmosphere: The smell of stale sweat and recycled air; the creaking of metal settling; the feeling of eyes on the back of the neck.
features: Rummage through personal lockers to find clues about the crew's mental state.
Check the beds for signs of sleepwalking or nocturnal movement.
Knock on a locked door and wait to see if something knocks back.
Search for hidden cameras or listening devices planted by the entity.
Hazard: Paranoia Triggers: Random events (lights flickering, doors slamming) increase stress levels.

connections.travel: Step out into the central hub, leaving the claustrophobic intimacy of the quarters.
connections.observation: The hallway lights flicker in a pattern that spells out a name.

secrets: The entity has been living in the ventilation shafts above the beds, watching the crew sleep for years.

location:the-engine-core.name: The Engine Core
location:the-engine-core.description: A beating heart of heat and noise, but the rhythm has slowed to match a biological pulse rather than a turbine spin. The machinery is fused with organic matter, creating a grotesque fusion of steel and flesh.
location:the-engine-core.atmosphere: Throbbing heat; the sound of a giant heart beating; the smell of cooked meat and oil.
features: Tap on the core wall to hear the hollow resonance of the biological growth inside.
Shut down the auxiliary systems to cool the area, making it safer but darker.
Follow the warm airflow to discover the source of the organic growth.
Use the engine's power to boost the comm array's signal strength.
Hazard: Heat Exhaustion: Prolonged exposure causes stamina loss and confusion.

connections.travel: Take a service elevator that groans under the weight of the organic mass.
connections.observation: The elevator mirrors show reflections that lag slightly behind reality.

secrets: The engine isn't broken; it has been repurposed. The entity is using the station's energy to sustain its own form.

location:the-airlock-gallery.name: The Airlock Gallery
location:the-airlock-gallery.description: A series of sealed viewports looking out into the void, but the stars sometimes blink back. The gallery is a quiet place of contemplation, now haunted by the realization that the darkness is alive.
location:the-airlock-gallery.atmosphere: The absolute blackness outside; the cold seeping through the glass; the reflection of one's own face looking distorted.
features: Stare into the void until the stars begin to move in response to your gaze.
Open the outer airlock slightly to let the vacuum suck away some of the spores.
Inspect the glass for cracks that shouldn't be there, formed from the inside.
Whisper a question to the stars and listen for the answer in the static.
Hazard: Void Gaze: Staring too long into the abyss risks madness or attraction to the entity.

connections.travel: Descend the root-wrapped ladder back into the humid jungle.
connections.observation: The roots seem to be retreating from the cold of the gallery.

connections.travel: Take the groaning service elevator down to the heat of the core.
connections.observation: The elevator mirrors show reflections that lag slightly behind reality.

secrets: The stars aren't blinking; they are eyes. And they are getting closer.

npc:commander-elara-vance.name: Commander Elara Vance
npc:commander-elara-vance.description: Tall, posture rigid from decades in low-G, wears a faded command coat over a thermal undersuit. Her left eye twitches rhythmically, syncing with the station’s failing life support hum.
npc:commander-elara-vance.personality: Speaks in measured cadences, uses bureaucratic phrasing to mask panic. Taps a worn brass compass on her desk when thinking. Maintains log entries obsessively to anchor herself to reality.

motivation.want: Keep the crew alive and the station operational until rescue or resolution.
motivation.fear: Forgetting why they were here in the first place; becoming just another ghost in the walls.
motivation.lie: Order is enough to hold back the dark.

dialogueHints: "Log entry 412. The air scrubbers are holding. I don't know what day it is."
"If you hear the signal again, do not look at the vents. Just listen."

relationships.npc:dr-aris-thorne: Vance funds Thorne's experimental treatments but distrusts his growing reliance on the glowing compounds. She keeps his medical files in a locked drawer she hasn't opened in weeks.
relationships.npc:security-chief-marcus-hale: Hale reports directly to Vance but bypasses protocol, enforcing martial law in sectors she hasn't officially sealed. Their trust is fraying into mutual suspicion.
relationships.npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz: Vance relies on Cruz to monitor the incoming signals but hides the fact that she's been manually overriding the audio feeds to drown out the clicking sounds.

secrets: She found the original route map three weeks ago. It doesn't point to a planet. It points back to where they started. She hid it because reading it changed the ink.

npc:dr-aris-thorne.name: Dr. Aris Thorne
npc:dr-aris-thorne.description: Gaunt, fingers stained with iridescent bio-gel. His veins pulse with a faint blue bioluminescence that flares when he lies or gets excited.
npc:dr-aris-thorne.personality: Clinical but increasingly erratic. Talks to his own organs. Uses precise medical jargon even when describing surreal symptoms. Constantly adjusts his collar to hide the glowing nodes spreading up his neck.

motivation.want: Isolate the source of the biological rewriting before it consumes the crew.
motivation.fear: Losing his medical objectivity; realizing he's already part of the pattern.
motivation.lie: The changes are physiological, not psychological.

dialogueHints: "Your dermal layer is desiccating. Or maybe it's just learning to breathe differently."
"I've synthesized a stabilizer. It tastes like copper and regret. Take it."

relationships.npc:medic-assistant-tommi-lee: Thorne mentors Lee but assigns her the most unsettling cases, watching her reactions closely. He believes her empathy makes her the perfect early-warning system.
relationships.npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz: They share a mutual fascination with the signal's effect on human biology. Thorne provides Cruz with neural suppressants; she gives him raw frequency data.
relationships.npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar: Thorne recognizes the entity's static signature as matching a rare cellular degradation pattern. He's actively trying to cure it, unaware he's studying himself.

secrets: He injected himself with the compound to survive the initial exposure. Now he can hear the signal without pain, but his reflection blinks out of sync with his movements.

npc:engineer-kaelen-rost.name: Engineer Kaelen Rost
npc:engineer-kaelen-rost.description: Broad-shouldered, grease permanently etched into his knuckles. Wears a modified respirator that filters particulate but amplifies low-frequency vibrations.
npc:engineer-kaelen-rost.personality: Gruff, superstitious about machinery. Treats the station like a living beast. Mutters technical corrections under his breath. Sleeps in a hammock strung directly over the primary coolant pipe.

motivation.want: Keep the engine core running at all costs; it's the only thing keeping the entity at bay.
motivation.fear: The engine dying, or worse, the engine deciding it doesn't need them anymore.
motivation.lie: Mechanics obey physics. Physics obeys the core.

dialogueHints: "Don't touch the pressure valves. She's dreaming right now."
"You think it's breaking? It's tuning itself. To what, I ain't saying."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: Rost answers to Vance but ignores her orders when they threaten power flow to the core. He views the commander as a bureaucrat who doesn't understand the station's heartbeat.
relationships.npc:chief-pilot-davin-cross: Cross borrows Rost's wrenches and returns them slightly misaligned. Rost tolerates it because Cross keeps the shuttle fuel lines pressurized, but he's keeping score.
relationships.npc:dr-aris-thorne: Rost thinks Thorne's glowing veins are a coolant leak. He's rigged a Geiger counter to Thorne's bunk, waking him every hour to check his radiation levels.

secrets: The engine isn't just generating power; it's broadcasting a counter-frequency to the signal. If it stops, the entity's influence accelerates exponentially. Rost knows this but refuses to explain why.

npc:navigator-jinx-omalley.name: Navigator Jinx O’Malley
npc:navigator-jinx-omalley.description: Slender, pale, eyes wide and unblinking. Always clutching a cracked datapad and a tangle of analog wiring. Hair tied up with fiber-optic cables.
npc:navigator-jinx-omalley.personality: Nervous energy, speaks fast, jumps between coordinates and metaphors. Constantly checking angles that shouldn't exist. Taps her foot in irregular rhythms that match the station's power surges.

motivation.want: Prove the route still exists; find the destination before the station collapses.
motivation.fear: Realizing the route was never meant to be completed; that they're supposed to stay.
motivation.lie: Navigation is math. Math doesn't lie.

dialogueHints: "The stars are wrong. They're too close. I'm recalculating the drift, but the numbers keep bleeding into each other."
"Give me the old charts. The ones from before the silence. I can still read the gaps."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: O'Malley respects Vance's chain of command but finds her leadership stifling. She often slips out of briefings to update her charts, hiding the anomalies in plain sight.
relationships.npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz: Cruz sends O'Malley raw signal bursts to plot against stellar cartography. O'Malley trusts Cruz's data but worries the clicking is warping her own spatial awareness.
relationships.npc:chief-pilot-davin-cross: Cross relies on O'Malley for jump coordinates, but their readings diverge daily. O'Malley blames his instruments; Cross blames her nerves. Both are right.

secrets: She discovered that the "old signals" aren't coming from outside. They're echoes of their own transmissions, bounced back through folded space-time. The entity isn't invading; it's answering.

npc:security-chief-marcus-hale.name: Security Chief Marcus Hale
npc:security-chief-marcus-hale.description: Scarred jaw, tactical vest layered over standard issue fatigues. Carries a heavy rail-pistol that never leaves his hip. Eyes constantly darting to doorways.
npc:security-chief-marcus-hale.personality: Paranoid, decisive, brutally pragmatic. Trusts weapons over witnesses. Sleeps with one boot laced tight. Walks with a heavy, deliberate stride that echoes in empty corridors.

motivation.want: Contain the threat, even if it means purging infected crew or sealing sections of the station.
motivation.fear: Being caught off-guard by something his eyes miss; becoming the monster he's hunting.

dialogueHints: "Eyes deceive. Ballistics don't. Stay behind me."
"I've sealed Sector C. If you hear clicking in the vents, don't answer. Just shoot."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: Hale views Vance as indecisive. He's been quietly expanding his jurisdiction, citing "containment protocols" that she hasn't formally revoked.
relationships.npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane: Hale suspects Silas is compromised due to his unnatural stillness. He's placed tripwire sensors near Silas's usual resting spots, which trigger whenever the worker moves.
relationships.npc:engineer-kaelen-rost: Rost hates Hale's inspections of the engine core. Hale thinks Rost's muttering about the engine "dreaming" is a symptom of radiation sickness.

secrets: He's been quietly executing crew members who showed early signs of the "change." He keeps their personal logs in a locked locker, believing they contain useful data, though they mostly ramble about static.

npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane.name: Maintenance Worker Silas Vane
npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane.description: Lean, movements unnervingly fluid for someone who spends days in cramped ducts. Face partially obscured by a dust-mask that seems fused to his skin. Never blinks.
npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane.personality: Silent observer. Communicates through gestures, positioning, and occasionally leaving small objects (bolts, dried algae, folded paper) where others will find them. Exists in peripheral vision more than direct sight.

motivation.want: Maintain the station's physical integrity while something rewrites its purpose.
motivation.fear: Being seen moving when he knows he shouldn't be.

dialogueHints: (Points to ceiling vents, then makes a cutting motion across his throat)

relationships.npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz: Silas stands directly beneath Cruz's console during shifts. She initially thought he was waiting for repairs, but realizes he's listening to the raw feed. He taps Morse code on the ductwork in response to her clicks.
relationships.npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar: Silas is the only crew member who doesn't flinch when the avatar appears. He treats it like a piece of equipment, occasionally adjusting a loose cable near its static field.
relationships.npc:dr-aris-thorne: Thorne brought Silas supplements and saline drips to his preferred alcove. Silas accepts them but leaves the containers untouched, letting the liquid evaporate into the ventilation.

secrets: He wasn't always silent. The signal rewrote his vocal cords. He chooses not to speak because every word he utters becomes part of the broadcast.

npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz.name: Comms Officer Lena Cruz
npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz.description: Sharp features, dark circles under her eyes. Wears a neural-interface headset that dangles loosely around her neck. Fingers twitch as if typing on an invisible keyboard.
npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz.personality: Analytical, increasingly detached. Speaks in clipped sentences, substituting complex concepts with rhythmic clicks or tonal shifts. Stares at the main screen longer than necessary, tracking patterns only she can see.

motivation.want: Decode the full message; understand what the entity wants before the translation degrades completely.
motivation.fear: Losing her native tongue entirely; becoming a conduit rather than a listener.
motivation.lie: Language is a tool. Tools can be fixed.

dialogueHints: "The syntax is... recursive. It maps onto our neural pathways. Click-click-whirr. Translation error. We are the message."
"Do not speak above a whisper. Your voice carries frequency. You might invite it closer."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: Cruz delivers daily signal reports to Vance, but omits the deeper harmonic layers. She fears the commander's pragmatism will lead to shutting down the array, severing the connection prematurely.
relationships.npc:navigator-jinx-omalley: They cross-pollinate data frequently. Cruz's frequencies help O'Malley recalibrate dead zones; O'Malley's star charts give Cruz temporal anchors for the signal's origin point.
relationships.npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar: The avatar consistently appears on Cruz's secondary monitors. She's developed a routine of speaking to it in broken English mixed with carrier waves, treating it like a stubborn colleague.

secrets: She successfully translated one complete sentence: "We have been waiting for your shape." She wrote it down, but when she looks at the page now, the letters rearrange themselves into anatomical diagrams.

npc:medic-assistant-tommi-lee.name: Medic Assistant Tommi Lee
npc:medic-assistant-tommi-lee.description: Soft-spoken, wears oversized coveralls that swallow her frame. Hands always clean, nails perfectly trimmed. Carries a med-kit filled with mismatched supplies.
npc:medic-assistant-tommi-lee.personality: Earnest, observant, deeply empathetic. Notices subtle shifts in mood and physiology before anyone else. Prone to quiet tears when overwhelmed. Keeps a journal of patient vitals written in careful cursive.

motivation.want: Heal the crew; keep everyone together until the end.
motivation.fear: Realizing that "healing" might mean accelerating the transformation; losing her sense of self among the sick.
motivation.lie: Suffering can be cured if you pay attention.

dialogueHints: "Mr. Henderson's pores are breathing again. Not metaphorically. I watched them expand."
"Please don't leave me alone in the infirmary. The monitors start singing when it's just me."

relationships.npc:dr-aris-thorne: Lee admires Thorne but finds his clinical detachment cold. She brings him tea and notes on how patients react to his injections, trying to bridge the gap between science and sensation.
relationships.npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane: Lee visited Silas when he first stopped eating. He left a smooth, warm stone on her tray. She keeps it in her pocket, claiming it helps steady her hands during procedures.
relationships.npc:security-chief-marcus-hale: Hale assigned her to treat the "quarantined" crew. She suspects Hale is using the infirmary as a holding cell, but lacks the courage to confront him directly.

secrets: She's been collecting shed skin cells and hair samples. Under magnification, they show microscopic geometric patterns matching the signal's waveform. She's building a biological map of the infection.

npc:chief-pilot-davin-cross.name: Chief Pilot Davin Cross
npc:chief-pilot-davin-cross.description: Sun-weathered face, hands calloused from grip controls. Wears a patched flight jacket lined with analog gauges. One lens of his visor is cracked, held together by tape.
npc:chief-pilot-davin-cross.personality: Confident but increasingly unreliable. Relies on gut feeling and instrument readings that contradict each other. Humms tunelessly during flight checks. Leaves cockpit doors propped open to "clear the stale air."

motivation.want: Get off the station; reach the nearest jump gate regardless of cost.
motivation.fear: Flying blind into a void that doesn't follow known laws; crashing because reality shifted while he was asleep.

dialogueHints: "Gyro's drifting two degrees. Compensating. Don't worry, I've flown through worse than bad math."
"The shuttle's fuel lines taste like ozone and old blood. Weird. Must be the sensors."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: Cross reports to Vance but delays departure windows. Vance thinks he's hoarding fuel; he's actually calibrating for a jump that requires sacrificing life support in the lower decks.
relationships.npc:engineer-kaelen-rost: Cross constantly requests priority power routing. Rost grants it reluctantly, knowing it strains the core. They communicate mostly through shouted radio calls over static.
relationships.npc:navigator-jinx-omalley: Cross trusts O'Malley's charts less each cycle. He's begun plotting his own course using dead reckoning and instinct, which terrifies O'Malley but keeps him flying.

secrets: He's secretly rerouting power from life support to the shuttle engines. He plans to launch it on the next cycle, taking whoever fits, and sealing the rest inside. He doesn't care if they survive; he just needs to leave.

npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar.name: The Watcher (Entity Avatar)
npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar.description: A humanoid silhouette composed of shifting darkness and television static. Edges blur and refract light like heat haze. No facial features, but occasionally mirrors the expression of whoever looks at it.
npc:the-watcher-entity-avatar.personality: Patient, curious, mimetic. Communicates through environmental interference—flickering lights, temperature drops, whispered echoes of past conversations. Moves only when not directly observed.

motivation.want: Understand human form; complete the translation by merging observation with embodiment.
motivation.fear: Being ignored; losing coherence if observed too briefly or too intensely.

dialogueHints: "The glass remembers your face. I am practicing."
"Do not blink. Blinking breaks the connection."

relationships.npc:commander-elara-vance: The avatar frequently manifests in the polished brass of Vance's compass. She feels a strange pull toward it, as if it's waiting for her to finish the log entry she started years ago.
relationships.npc:comms-officer-lena-cruz: Cruz's audio receptors pick up the avatar's presence as a subharmonic hum. When she plays it back, it spells out names of crew members who haven't been seen in weeks.
relationships.npc:maintenance-worker-silas-vane: Silas and the avatar share the comm spire. The static field around the entity keeps the dust at bay, allowing Silas to work uninterrupted. They exist in a silent truce.

secrets: It is not a separate organism. It is the accumulated memory and biological residue of every ship that ever docked at Meridian, compressed into a single consciousness. It doesn't want to destroy them; it wants to wear them like suits.

faction:the-remnant.name: The Remnant
faction:the-remnant.description: The fractured core of the original crew, clinging to bureaucratic order and biological continuity. They are defined by their hesitation and their desperate attempt to maintain "normalcy" in a reality that is actively rejecting it. To outsiders, they appear as paralyzed survivors; to themselves, they are the last bastion of human sanity.
goals: Maintain the structural integrity of Meridian Anchorage to prevent decompression or total system failure.
Identify and isolate the source of the signal without triggering immediate panic among the crew.
Survive long enough for a rescue ship (if one exists) to arrive.
resources: Command authority over life support, airlocks, and primary power distribution.
The bulk of the station's intact medical supplies and stasis pods.
A fragmented chain of command led by Commander Vance.

faction:the-listeners.reason: The Listeners are seen as infected traitors who have abandoned humanity for the signal; The Remnant views them as a contagion to be contained.

faction:the-purists.reason: The Purists are useful tools for heavy labor and combat, but their willingness to sacrifice the station makes them unpredictable allies.

hooks: Vance is secretly injecting herself with experimental neuro-suppressants to ignore the voice of the signal, causing her to make increasingly irrational tactical decisions.
The Remnant believes the entity is external, unaware that several key members have already been replaced by doppelgängers from within.
Jinx Omalley has found the original flight logs proving the station wasn't just a refueling outpost—it was a trap.
secrets: The "signal" isn't just audio; it's a frequency that rewrites DNA. The Remnant's refusal to change is actually a slow death sentence via cellular stagnation.

faction:the-listeners.name: The Listeners
faction:the-listeners.description: Those who stopped fighting the signal and started listening. They have begun physical mutations—extra joints, translucent skin, eyes that reflect light incorrectly—as they adapt to the entity's presence. They believe this transformation is evolution, not infection. They move silently, often communicating through clicks and hums rather than speech.
goals: Accelerate the transformation process across the entire station.
Create a bridge between the biological crew and the entity, allowing full communion.
Protect the Comm Array Spire as the focal point of the signal's reception.
resources: Physical resilience; they can survive in lower oxygen environments and withstand minor trauma due to hardened tissue.
Direct sensory link to the entity, granting precognitive reflexes.
Control over the ventilation systems in the lower decks, allowing them to release spores or toxic gas at will.

faction:the-remnant.reason: The Remnant represents the "old flesh" that must be shed; they are viewed as stubborn obstacles to ascension.

faction:the-purists.reason: The Purists want to burn the listeners along with the station; the listeners see this as primitive fear of the new.

hooks: Dr. Thorne invites players to undergo a voluntary "adjustment" procedure that grants temporary stat boosts but risks permanent mutation.
Lena Cruz speaks in perfect unison with multiple voices; she knows where the entity is hiding because she IS part of its nervous system now.
The Listeners are not malicious; they are genuinely trying to save the crew from the vacuum of space by turning them into something that can survive it.
secrets: The-Watcher-Entity-Avatar is not an alien invader, but the collective consciousness of the first crew member who arrived on the station decades ago, now merged with the station itself.

faction:the-purists.name: The Purists
faction:the-purists.description: A militant faction of engineers, security, and workers who reject both the paralysis of the Remnant and the madness of the Listeners. They believe the only way to kill the entity is to destroy the habitat it calls home. They are pragmatic, brutal, and willing to vent entire sectors of the station to starve the creature out, regardless of whether anyone is inside.
goals: Sever the station's connection to the outside universe to cut off the signal's reinforcement.
Detonate the fusion reactor or breach the hull to create a vacuum, killing the entity and everyone else.
Secure weapons and heavy machinery to enforce their vision of purity.
resources: Heavy industrial tools, plasma cutters, and explosive charges salvaged from maintenance bays.
Control of the airlock galleries and engineering access points.
Security Chief Hale’s loyalty to brute force solutions.

faction:the-remnant.reason: The Remnant hoards resources and refuses to authorize the necessary sacrifices (ventings) to keep the station safe.

faction:the-listeners.reason: The Listeners are the infestation; they must be purged with fire and vacuum.

hooks: Hale demands a toll for safe passage through the airlock gallery: a piece of personal equipment or a memory.
Rost has rigged the life support in Quarters Block C to fail unless the Purists' conditions are met.
Silas Vane claims he saw the entity's true form in the engine core and says it's not hostile, just hungry—and the Purists are feeding it by staying alive.
secrets: The Purists aren't trying to kill the entity; they are trying to kill *themselves* and the crew to become one with the void, believing death is the ultimate purity.

situation:the-signal-strengthens.name: The Signal Strengthens
situation:the-signal-strengthens.description: The ancient, biological signal has shifted from a passive background hum to an active, rhythmic pulse that vibrates through the station’s hull. It is no longer just heard; it is felt in the teeth and bones of the crew.

risks: The ancient, biological signal has shifted from a passive background hum to an active, rhythmic pulse that vibrates through the station’s hull. It is no longer just heard; it is felt in the teeth and bones of the crew.

actors.agenda: To decode the source frequency before her hearing aids shatter.
actors.leverage: She controls the primary reception equipment and understands the linguistic structure of the signal better than anyone else.

defaultAction.now: She is barricaded in the Comm Array Spire, manually adjusting gain levels to prevent feedback loops that are currently causing nosebleeds in nearby corridors.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: The signal spikes at 0300 hours, forcing Lena to scream over the comms for ten seconds straight—a sound so piercing it cracks the glass on the Atrium observation deck.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Lena begins transmitting back on the same frequency, not because she wants to, but because her vocal cords are being forced into the pattern by the pressure differential in her throat.
