actors.agenda: Maintain order while the crew’s grip on reality slips.
actors.leverage: Command authority and control over ration distribution.

defaultAction.now: Vance is issuing mandatory sedative rations to all shifts, claiming it’s for “fatigue,” but actually trying to keep everyone quiet enough to hear the signal’s origin point.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: She locks down Quarters Block C, declaring it a quarantine zone after finding three crew members standing perfectly still in their bunks, smiling with their eyes open.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Vance orders the engine core to be flooded with coolant to dampen the vibrations, sacrificing life support efficiency to buy silence, angering the Engineering team.

clock.name: The Resonance Threshold
clock.doom: The signal becomes audible even through sealed helmets and bone-conduction implants. The Entity can now directly project thoughts into the minds of every living thing onboard simultaneously.
clock.pauseCondition: The party successfully identifies the modulation key of the signal and jams it using the Comm Array, or physically destroys the external antenna dish.

stages.name: The Hum
stages.description: A low-level vibration that causes minor insomnia and irritability.

consequences.narrative: Crew members report headaches and the taste of copper in their mouths.

stages.name: The Pulse
stages.description: The sound becomes rhythmic, matching the heartbeat of those who listen too long.

trigger.eventDescription: Lena fails a WIT check to stabilize the audio feed.

consequences.narrative: People start tapping their feet or nodding their heads involuntarily in time with the noise.

stages.name: The Voice
stages.description: The signal resolves into recognizable, albeit alien, phonemes.

consequences.narrative: Three random NPCs begin speaking in unison during meals, repeating the same phrase.

stages.name: The Chorus
stages.description: Every surface in the station seems to vibrate with the sound.

consequences.narrative: Structural integrity warnings flash across all screens; glass is cracking.

outgoingLeads.information: The signal’s frequency matches the bio-electric rhythm of the human brain, specifically the theta waves associated with deep REM sleep.

discovery.description: Lena shows the waveform graph to a PC with sufficient Wit, pointing out the uncanny resemblance to neural activity.

outgoingLeads.information: The vibrations are strongest near Hydroponics Bay 4, suggesting the signal is not coming from space, but from *inside* the station’s biology.

discovery.description: PCs notice that dust motes in the Atrium dance in complex geometric patterns when the signal pulses, tracing a path toward Hydroponics.

outgoingLeads.information: Archived logs from twenty years ago mention a “biological anomaly” in the hydroponics sector that was dismissed as mold.

discovery.description: Found in the digital archives of the Atrium terminal, buried under layers of maintenance reports.

victory.description: The The Signal Strengthens is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Signal Strengthens ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The ancient, biological signal has shifted from a passive background hum to an active, rhythmic pulse that vibrates through the station’s hull. It is no longer just heard; it is felt in the teeth and bones of the crew.

situation:life-support-failing.name: Life Support Failing
situation:life-support-failing.description: The plants in Hydroponics Bay 4 have grown out of control, bursting through their containment fields. Their roots are now clogging the oxygen scrubbers and siphoning carbon dioxide at an accelerated rate, creating a localized dead zone.

risks: The plants in Hydroponics Bay 4 have grown out of control, bursting through their containment fields. Their roots are now clogging the oxygen scrubbers and siphoning carbon dioxide at an accelerated rate, creating a localized dead zone.

actors.agenda: Keep the engines running, even if it means burning the plant matter for fuel.
actors.leverage: Control over the power grid and thermal systems.

defaultAction.now: Rost is manually overriding the temperature regulators in Hydroponics, trying to freeze the growth, but the plants generate their own heat.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Rost decides to vent the atmosphere in Hydroponics to starve the plants of oxygen, which will reduce the station’s total air supply by 15%.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Rost burns the remaining plant mass in the incinerators, releasing thick, spore-filled smoke that drifts into the ventilation system, affecting the whole station.

actors.agenda: Save his sister, who is trapped in the deeper sections of the bay.
actors.leverage: Knowledge of the maintenance shafts and bypass routes.

defaultAction.now: Silas is hiding in the upper gantries of Hydroponics, watching his sister argue with the plants below.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: He cuts the main oxygen line to the bay to force the plants to retreat, causing a pressure drop that ruptures pipes in the adjacent corridor.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: He brings his sister out, but she is partially fused with a root system, breathing through gills that haven’t been there before.

clock.name: Asphyxiation Clock
clock.doom: Oxygen levels drop below 10%. All crew suffer from severe hypoxia, leading to unconsciousness and eventual death unless emergency suits are donned immediately.
clock.pauseCondition: The party clears the blockage in the scrubbers, kills the root network, or seals off Hydroponics completely.

stages.name: Stuffy Air
stages.description: CO2 levels rise slightly; people feel drowsy.

consequences.narrative: Conversations become sluggish; errors increase in engineering reports.

stages.name: Shortness of Breath
stages.description: Physical exertion becomes difficult; heart rates elevate.

trigger.eventDescription: The root network expands into the secondary intake valve.

consequences.narrative: Crew members are seen gasping for air even while sitting still.

stages.name: Hallucinations
stages.description: The neurotoxin from the plants mixes with low oxygen, causing vivid delusions.

consequences.narrative: NPCs begin talking to the plants as if they are alive and speaking back.

stages.name: Crushing Dark
stages.description: Oxygen critical. Vision tunnels. Panic sets in.

consequences.narrative: Doors lock automatically due to sensor errors; people are trapped in rooms.

outgoingLeads.information: The roots are not just growing; they are actively moving toward the warmest parts of the station, specifically the Engine Core.

discovery.description: A PC notices the roots retracting from cold zones and extending rapidly toward the engine room vents.

outgoingLeads.information: Silas’s sister is not sick; she is being integrated. Her skin has taken on the texture of bark, and her veins are green.

discovery.description: Silas reveals this secret to a PC he trusts, showing them the spot where her arm merged with the root.

outgoingLeads.information: The spores released by the plants match the cellular structure of the Entity mentioned in old logs.

discovery.description: Tommi analyzes a sample of the spores and finds DNA markers identical to the “anomaly” from decades ago.

victory.description: The Life Support Failing is resolved successfully

failure.description: The Life Support Failing ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The plants in Hydroponics Bay 4 have grown out of control, bursting through their containment fields. Their roots are now clogging the oxygen scrubbers and siphoning carbon dioxide at an accelerated rate, creating a localized dead zone.

situation:identity-crisis.name: Identity Crisis
situation:identity-crisis.description: Crew members are experiencing severe memory fragmentation. They forget names, faces, and even basic skills. Some claim to be someone else entirely, or to have never boarded the station.

risks: Crew members are experiencing severe memory fragmentation. They forget names, faces, and even basic skills. Some claim to be someone else entirely, or to have never boarded the station.

actors.agenda: Find a medical cause for the amnesia before Vance declares martial law.
actors.leverage: Access to medical records and sedatives.

defaultAction.now: Tommi is administering high-dose stimulants to patients who are catatonic, trying to shock their memories back online.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Tommi discovers that the amnesia is correlated with exposure to the signal, not the plants, and starts injecting the crew with blockers.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Tommi realizes they themselves are forgetting who they are, and begins writing notes to themselves that they won’t remember reading later.

actors.agenda: Prove that they are still the Navigator, not just a passenger.
actors.leverage: Knowledge of the station’s layout and escape routes.

defaultAction.now: Jinx is wandering the halls, checking maps that look wrong to everyone else, insisting the station has more decks than it should.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Jinx locks themselves in the Navigation Deck, refusing to let anyone in until they “remember the way home.”
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Jinx opens the door, but their eyes are gone—replaced by smooth, black orbs that reflect the station’s interior.

clock.name: Erosion of Self
clock.doom: The crew forgets what humans are. They stand still for days, waiting for instructions from the Entity, ready to be harvested or transformed.
clock.pauseCondition: The party identifies the specific frequency of the signal that triggers the memory loss and creates a counter-frequency.

stages.name: Foggy
stages.description: Minor forgetfulness; misplacing tools, forgetting names.

consequences.narrative: People call each other by the wrong names.

stages.name: Fragmented
stages.description: Memory gaps last hours; skills are forgotten (e.g., how to use a weapon).

trigger.eventDescription: A crew member fails to recognize their own reflection.

consequences.narrative: Combat proficiency drops; social bonds fray as people forget shared history.

stages.name: Empty
stages.description: Personality traits vanish; crew becomes blank slates.

consequences.narrative: NPCs stop speaking and stare at the walls, drooling slightly.

stages.name: Replaced
stages.description: The Entity’s consciousness overlays the crew’s mind.

consequences.narrative: The crew moves in perfect unison, like a single organism.

outgoingLeads.information: The faces people see in mirrors are changing slowly, shifting toward the features of the Entity.

discovery.description: A PC notices their reflection blinking when they don’t.

outgoingLeads.information: All affected crew members are having the same dream: a vast, dark ocean where something is swimming beneath them.

discovery.description: Tommi collects dream journals from patients and reads them aloud to the party.

outgoingLeads.information: The numbers on the doors in Quarters Block C are changing every time you look away, spelling out coordinates.

discovery.description: A PC with high Observation notices the digits rearranging themselves.

victory.description: The Identity Crisis is resolved successfully

failure.description: The Identity Crisis ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Crew members are experiencing severe memory fragmentation. They forget names, faces, and even basic skills. Some claim to be someone else entirely, or to have never boarded the station.

situation:the-entity-awakens.name: The Entity Awakens
situation:the-entity-awakens.description: Movement is detected in the deepest part of the station, near the Airlock Gallery. Something massive is unfolding itself from the shadows, drawn by the weakened state of the crew.

risks: Movement is detected in the deepest part of the station, near the Airlock Gallery. Something massive is unfolding itself from the shadows, drawn by the weakened state of the crew.

actors.agenda: Protect the Commander at all costs.

defaultAction.now: Hale is setting up a perimeter around the Commander’s quarters, armed with a pulse rifle.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Hale engages a scout drone sent by the Entity, only to find the drone’s camera feed showed the drone being eaten by its own shadow.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Hale sacrifices himself to hold off the first wave of the Entity’s spawn, buying time but losing the security wing.

actors.agenda: To rejoin with its scattered consciousness and consume the station.
actors.leverage: Superior strength, regeneration, and ability to manipulate the environment.

defaultAction.now: The Avatar is coalescing in the Airlock Gallery, forming a shape that resembles a giant, pale eye surrounded by tendrils.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: It extends a tendril into the Engine Core, seizing control of the life support systems remotely.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: The Avatar detaches a portion of itself to hunt the PCs, while the main body begins to breach the outer hull.

clock.name: The Unfolding
clock.doom: The Entity breaches the outer hull, exposing the station to vacuum. The crew is either sucked out or consumed inside.
clock.pauseCondition: The party destroys the Avatar’s physical form or severs its connection to the station’s power grid.

stages.name: Shadows
stages.description: Movement detected in periphery vision.

consequences.narrative: CCTV footage shows static in the corners of the Airlock Gallery.

stages.name: Whispers
stages.description: The Entity speaks directly to individuals.

trigger.eventDescription: A crew member goes missing in the Airlock Gallery.

consequences.narrative: People hear their name being called from the vents.

stages.name: Form
stages.description: A physical shape is visible in the Gallery.

consequences.narrative: The lights in the Gallery flicker and die.

stages.name: Breach
stages.description: The Entity tears through the hull plating.

consequences.narrative: Atmosphere begins to vent; gravity fails in the lower decks.

outgoingLeads.information: The Entity recognizes the PCs. It knows their names.

discovery.description: During the initial encounter, the Avatar speaks the PCs’ true names.

outgoingLeads.information: The Entity is not just inside; it is trying to get *out*, implying it was once part of the stars outside.

discovery.description: PCs see the Entity pressing against the glass of the gallery window, looking out at the void.

outgoingLeads.information: The Entity is drawing power from the Engine Core to sustain its form.

discovery.description: Rost notices a spike in energy consumption from the Airlock Gallery sector.

victory.description: The The Entity Awakens is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Entity Awakens ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Movement is detected in the deepest part of the station, near the Airlock Gallery. Something massive is unfolding itself from the shadows, drawn by the weakened state of the crew.

situation:mutiny-brewing.name: Mutiny Brewing
situation:mutiny-brewing.description: A faction of the crew, led by the Chief Pilot, believes that surrendering to the Entity is the only way to survive. They view the Entity as a higher form of life.

risks: A faction of the crew, led by the Chief Pilot, believes that surrendering to the Entity is the only way to survive. They view the Entity as a higher form of life.

actors.agenda: Lead the crew to “ascension” by joining the Entity.
actors.leverage: Control of the shuttle bay and the station’s escape vehicles.

defaultAction.now: Cross is giving impassioned speeches in the Atrium, preaching that the Entity is a gift, not a threat.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Cross kidnaps the Commander and holds her hostage in the Shuttle Bay, demanding she witness the “truth.”
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Cross launches a shuttle with a dozen followers, not to escape, but to crash into the Entity’s core to deliver a “message.”

actors.agenda: Prevent the mutiny from destabilizing the station.
actors.leverage: Legitimacy and command of the security forces.

defaultAction.now: Vance is trying to negotiate with Cross, offering him the title of First Officer if he stands down.
defaultAction.ifIgnored1Step: Vance orders Hale to arrest Cross, sparking a firefight in the Atrium.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Vance is captured by the mutineers and forced to watch as they prepare the shuttles for launch.

clock.name: The Takeover
clock.doom: The mutineers take full control of the station. The loyalists are executed or locked in the airlocks. The shuttles are launched, carrying the infected crew into the void.
clock.pauseCondition: The party exposes Cross’s madness, defeats him in combat, or convinces the crew that the Entity is hostile.

stages.name: Dissent
stages.description: Crew members question Vance’s leadership.

consequences.narrative: Graffiti appears in the mess hall: “LISTEN TO THE VOICE.”

stages.name: Defection
stages.description: Key personnel switch sides.

trigger.eventDescription: Cross gains the support of Engineering.

consequences.narrative: The life support systems are adjusted to favor the mutineers’ quarters.

stages.name: Seizure
stages.description: Cross takes the Command Center.

consequences.narrative: Vance is confined to her quarters.

stages.name: Ascension
stages.description: The shuttles are prepped for launch.

consequences.narrative: The airlocks cycle open, inviting the crew out.

outgoingLeads.information: Cross isn’t crazy; he’s right about the Entity, but wrong about the solution.

discovery.description: Cross reveals his knowledge of the Entity’s nature to a PC during a private conversation.

outgoingLeads.information: The shuttles are modified to withstand the Entity’s radiation, meaning Cross has known about it for weeks.

discovery.description: PCs find Cross’s log entries detailing the modifications.

outgoingLeads.information: The loyalists are afraid of Cross, not the Entity.

discovery.description: Hale mentions that Cross has been acting strangely even before the signal got loud.

openingScenes: The central comms console flickers to life during a routine cycle. A low, rhythmic pulse fills the deck plates—not mechanical, but wet and organic. Chief Comms Officer Aris Vane recognizes the cadence immediately, though she hasn’t heard it since childhood: her mother’s breathing pattern, recorded on a dead planet three generations ago. When she plays it back, the audio has evolved. It now contains the exact timbre of every voice currently within fifty meters.

A maintenance drone drifts back through Bay 4 with its magnetic clamps fused shut. When Engineering cuts it open, the interior isn’t filled with spare parts—it’s packed with human teeth arranged in a perfect spiral, still warm, still clicking against each other in time with the station’s new gravity tides. The blood inside the casing smells like ozone and old rain.

Safety cameras catch a figure standing in the observation blister, staring out at the void. The timestamp reads four hours ago. The figure turns toward the lens, and its jaw unhinges vertically to emit a sound that cracks the reinforced glass. The crew realizes the entity isn’t trying to get in; it’s practicing how to wear them.
plotHooks: The primary relay array is transmitting a reply on its own. Biometric scans show the signal originates from the crew’s own nervous systems. One player must climb the exterior gantry during a pressure drop to manually sever the hardline, knowing the entity will try to hold them there.

Hydroponics reports the nutrient vats are producing a sweet, fibrous growth that pulses when spoken to. The station’s medical officer insists it’s safe, but those who taste it begin dreaming in reverse. Investigate the water reclamation core where the first breach occurred to find the source strain.

Twin shifts have reported waking up in each other’s quarters with identical bruises. Their DNA sequencers show cellular migration—organs shifting position to accommodate new internal structures. Decide whether to quarantine them or let the transformation complete to gain a temporary biological advantage.

The station’s archival drive is rewriting mission logs. Every entry now ends with the same coordinate sequence, but the numbers keep changing based on the crew’s collective heart rates. Follow the coordinates to the long-forgotten cargo hold to discover what the “route” was actually leading to.
commonConflicts: Paranoia in the Dark: Crew members begin hoarding supplies, misplacing tools, or locking doors from the inside. The entity feeds on fractured trust, causing bulkheads to seal randomly and emergency lights to strobe in sync with rising panic. Players must perform coordinated actions under sensory distortion to maintain group cohesion.

Man Against Shifting Geometry: Corridors stretch, compress, and fold based on the signal’s frequency. Doors lead to rooms that no longer exist in the current timeline. Navigation requires sacrificing personal comfort or bodily integrity (e.g., blinding oneself to rely on touch, cutting away fused skin to pass through narrow gaps).

Communication Versus Assimilation: Understanding the message requires opening the mind and body to its influence. Each successful decoding grants profound knowledge or tactical advantages, but accelerates physiological rewriting. Players must choose between preserving their humanity or becoming fluent in the entity’s deep-time language.
resolutionPatterns: The Silence Returns: Players successfully isolate or destroy the biological conduit (relay array, core, or host). The signal ceases, the station stabilizes, and the entity recedes into dormant stasis. Survivors escape or remain, but all carry permanent tinnitus—a faint, wet whisper that only they can hear when alone.

Becoming the Beacon: The crew accepts the message as a gift rather than an invasion. Their bodies fully integrate with the station’s systems, transforming Meridian Anchorage into a living archive. Individual identities dissolve into a shared consciousness that broadcasts the crew’s memories across light-years, waiting for the next travelers to listen.

The Route is Walked: Decoding the final coordinate reveals the destination isn’t spatial but temporal. The station begins folding inward, shedding its physical hull like a chrysalis. Players must decide whether to step through the rupture into whatever lies beyond the edge of charted reality, leaving the dying station to drift as an empty monument.
